title: A Great Tool for my practice and probably useable for a show.
comment:
This device is a fantastic value for the money. I'm a user. Not a company or a reviewer.

I actually can't believe how nice the quality is. Still stunned. Feels and looks like a $300+ device to me. I have tried all settings (and they are many) in the rhythm section and the effects. Everything seemed to work the way I expected. It does require patience. There is so much creative content in this device it can be daunting. I decided to simplify a bit. I have not used the looper yet and I have no plans for tweaking the pedal mixes or pedal order. There is already so much flexibility off the shelf that I don't see what I could add. Key point for me is that I need to practice and play guitar more. I'm quite sure it would be fun to play around with the settings, mixes, etc. but that takes away from my playing time. GREAT PRODUCT. VERY GLAD I CHOZE IT FROM THE PACK. DUMB LUCK.

title: TERRIBLE!
comment: The sound quality for this Bluetooth speaker in the absolutely horrible! Your ten-year-old might like it because it's sort of loud however, as the parent of the household it will drive you absolutely crazy wanting to throw it out of the window. Additionally I'm very dissatisfied with the way the speaker arrived! It did not come packaged in a brown Amazon box, instead we just happened to open the front door and there it just sitting there visible to any and everyone! Thank God we were home and opened the door otherwise someone would have walked off with the speaker as it clearly shows on the box exactly what it is! It arrived about 4 days early, the driver did not ring the doorbell as I work from home and was sitting right at my desk (although delivery instructions specified to leave it at the back door and ring the doorbell). More about the speaker itself, if you value good quality sound and are extremely irritated bye horrible rattling bass sound then do not buy the speaker for your child, yourself, or anyone, just simply not buy it.

title: Surprisingly good quality.
comment:
I'm really impressed with how this low-end model plays.

I expected something more like a child's toy, but this this one plays very well in tune.

For a few dollars more than some of the cheapo brands I've bought, I'm shocked at the difference in quality. I've spend nearly as much on plastic flutes that just never play a sweet note. The Yamaha 20 series actually plays, and it plays well enough.

I bought it to round out my gift card, and I'm pleased with this choice. It's something to stick in my pocket and take along wherever I go. A simple song flute, basically.

For the cost of a child's toy, this one is both educational and provides endless hours of entertainment. There's always another song to learn.

I rate 5 stars because I obviously knew I wasn't getting a concert grade instrument. I'm rating it based on what it actually is: a student model for first time players. And for that, it is 5 star quality. Everything I expected and more.

title: Clear little cell phone accessory
comment:
[[VIDEOID:3ca5fbe6eb954c02fde51feecb6760fb]] So, I frequent an karaoke/singing app called SmuleSing! and lately I have noticed a need for an external microphone to help my voice project over the background music and my guitar, when I choose to play, while recording. The microphone has helped a great deal with just that. I may also use it for my video reviews, which I'm still learning how to do, from now on as well. It has a small convenient clip which I attach to my shirt but you could easily clip it to a camera stand or something in front of you if you prefer. I use this with my iphone 5C, which doesn't have the best internal microphone, to enhance the sound. To block out interference and outside noise I also use headphones. To do that, I purchased a little aux splitter piece with fits into my phone jack. See the video uploaded for an up close look at the mic and splitter as well as a demo on the sound quality. Demo includes just speaking voice, just an instrument and voice over the instrument. This item was offered to me at a discounted rate to try it out and review it honestly and I feel that I have.

If you are feeling at all interested in listening to an entire acoustic song from the app which I have performed using this very attachment, feel free to follow this link: [...]
It is an acoustic cover of Sia's- Elastic heart. The apps sound enhancing features improve the sound quality even further.

title: This is a beautiful guitar
comment:
I bought this guitar as a christmas gift to myself and I couldn't be happier. This guitar is amazing.

I have been playing now for about two years on an Ibanez and an acoustic and this is what I have observed so far.

Pros,
-the look
-the depth and space between the frets makes it very easy to play, I have larger fingers/hands so this is important to me
-the range of tone is excellent and the pickups are amazing
-price, for what you get, this guitar is a steal
-Mine came pretuned with D'addaro strings
-The cutaway lets me get to all the frets without hitting the body
-I wouldn't say it's heavy but it has a sturdy weight, and it's well balanced. I guess it feels solid to me.
-Everything on this guitar makes me feel like it's built to last

Cons,
-The floyd rose makes is slightly difficult to palm mute. I have to play the guitar lower or I end up wah-ing it.
-The nut has system that "locks" the strings down. This is a part of the floyd rose to help keep the guitar in tune. However I like to play a range of music and this makes changing it do drop-D or drop-C a pain. You can't make big changes without an allen wrench. But you can make minor changes from the floyd

Overall I think you can't get a better electric guitar for the price. I went into guitar center and played around for hours and I like this guitar better than many of the 1,000 dollar guitars.

title: Pretty...But Not Really A Good Guitar String!
comment: I bought two sets of .009's, one yellow, one green, for a Les Paul Goldtop and a Les Paul that is Emerald Green.  Just wanted to try something different than Ernie Ball Strings.  Well, I'm here to say it again, "Balls Are Best".  The coating was gone after one session in the studio and I really couldn't tell a difference, soundwise, between the DR Neons and Ernie Balls.  Plus, they are way more expensive for one set of DR Neons than a set of Ernie Balls, Gibsons, Fenders, or any other number of strings.  I've got to remember that more expensive doesn't necessarily mean better.  Now the DR Neon Bass Strings are great, both in looks and sound!  But if you're just needing great sounding guitar strings, I'd stick with Ernie Ball, Fender, Gibson, or any of the rest of the many brands out there.  Sorry, DR, but I won't be buying these again.

title: It's just ok.
comment:
For a beginner set, it is fine and enough to get someone started with learning the basics of drumming. The pads are a little on the small side which makes it tough to not have stick collisions on the pads. If you get this set, you'll wish you had a little more drum pad real estate to work with, and I'm already considering an upgrade option. It's not the end of the world, but the set is certainly not worth 5 stars. Also this thing was heavy in a single box, which shipped via USPS. This should have shipped on UPS or FedEx as most USPS mail trucks don't have room for large packages, nor do they carry a dolly. Our mailman was none to pleased as he struggled to the get the box up to my doorstep.
Kit setup took a good solid hour and then some, so carve out 2 hours to be safe when assembling the kit.

All in all, if this is your first set and you're not sure if playing drums is something you'll like, then jump right in. Your wallet will thank you.

title: These are exactly what I wanted
comment:
Bought these for me and my daughter to learn the piano a bit better. They are understandably a bit tedious to apply but honestly not too terrible.

Wipe all your keys with alcohol and a lint free cloth or paper towel first. Use the wooden mini popsicle stick. It is about the width of the inside of the white keys and the black keys. Start with Middle C. Place the bottom third or so of the sticker gently on the stick. Make it as straight and as evenly in the middle as you can. Place the stick flat on the key and line the sides of the stick up with the sides of the key, then hold the stick down firmly (hard enough to press the key down). Then take another finger and firmly press on the top of the sticker and gently peel the stick out from under the sticker and press the sticker the rest of the way down.

I saw one user say they used masking tape to keep their stickers straight, which sounds like a good idea that I was too lazy to try. Instead, I used the 5 lines of the staff to eyeball it, and I'm perfectly happy with the results. Keep in mind that you will have to adjust for the amount the sticker goes down since it's hinging over the popsicle stick. Just line it up a hair over where it should be and when you press down it should line up fine.

It is a bit of a confusing perspective when you see the bass staff over the treble staff where they meet, but if you look at both pics, you can see why it's like that as the bottom end of the bass section is so much lower than the staff, and likewise for the top end of the treble. So as long as people keep that in mind, they shouldn't have any problems

title: For Specialized Uses and Practice But Watch Size
comment: These are for specialized uses and practice. They are not loud enough or capable of producing the wide variety of sounds that congas with shells obviously can. For this reason, I think these drums are not good for beginners because, when using them, a beginner will be missing the full experience, and playing techniques will not produce the same results on these. Another issue is size. Despite what the listing says, these drum heads measure a little less than 11.75 inches, and the 11.75 inches DOES NOT INCLUDE the metal frame. If you are trying to transport them, you will need a bag about 1.5 inches larger than the advertised width because of the frame.

title: Ended up basically discarding the mic... Using the stand for a real mic.
comment:
Context: I record vocals and I also work remotely, those are my two use cases.

This microphone should not be used for recording music. Maybe not even for just simple phone calls.. It's not great, and I was using it with a proper recording interface with phantom power too. Extremely quiet and sounds, really just bad.

HOWEVER: the stand is actually pretty sweet! The pop filter is fine for what it is. This microphone ended up just sitting in the corner of my room, and I hooked a real microphone (Shure SM7B) up to the stand that came with this package.

The stand is great! Actuates in every way it should, is very sturdy. Should work with any XLR mic, and even comes with some nice cable ties.

But steer clear from the actual microphone itself...

title: It won't stay up!
comment:
It’s a pretty sturdy stand. Not as easy to fold as I thought, but not a huge deal. The little metal arms that keep the pages down is my biggest complaint. You can move them up and down, but not all the way down. Part of the arm always sticks up and has twice ripped the bottom of the page. As long as you’re mindful of it though, it should be fine. My favorite part is the light. It’s what set it apart from other similar stand. The light has two setting and comes with a charger. The light can dim pretty quickly when it’s on the brightest setting, but you can use it while it’s charging, so there’s that.

Edit: I've had this stand for a little over two months. I originally rated it as 4 stars, but recently it has started falling down. Not falling over, it just won't stay at the height I set it; it keeps sliding all the way down. I don't know why but it is -extremely- frustrating and kinda defeats the purpose of the stand. It's a fine stand other than that, but it's becoming unusable.
